data.content: Here's the conversion. MSTest.Sdk replaces the standard SDK and automatically pulls in `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, so all three package references can be removed.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a standard MSTest v2 project. To convert to MSTest.Sdk:

1. Change the SDK from `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` to `MSTest.Sdk/3.8.2` (or similar latest version)
2. Remove the three MSTest/test-related package references — MSTest.Sdk pulls them in automatically
3. Keep `IsPackable` and `TargetFramework`

Let me make the conversion.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Restore succeeded. Here's what changed:

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` | `Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.2"` |
| 3 PackageReferences (TestFramework, TestAdapter, NET.Test.Sdk) | Removed — all implicit |
| `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>` | Removed — MSTest.Sdk sets this automatically |

The SDK version (`3.8.2`) pins the MSTest version. To upgrade later, just bump that number.
